title: Build a composed creature
prompt_md:
## Goal

Implement two classes, `Point2D` and `Creature`, where a `Creature` **composes** a `Point2D` describing its location. The point is a value member with no independent existence.

## Input

- Line 1: a name (single word, 1-50 characters).
- Line 2: two integers `x` and `y` (-1000 <= x, y <= 1000).

## Output

A single line: `<name> is at (<x>, <y>)`.

## Notes

- `Point2D` should expose its coordinates.
- `Creature` should hold a `Point2D` **value member** (not a pointer or reference) plus the name, and expose them. Build the output from those members.

title: Computer with composed parts
prompt_md:
## Goal

Model a `Computer` that **composes** a `CPU` and a `Memory` part as value members. Each part can describe itself; the computer assembles a summary from its parts.

## Input

- Line 1: a CPU model (single word) and an integer `cores` (1 <= cores <= 128).
- Line 2: an integer `sizeGB` (1 <= sizeGB <= 1024).

## Output

```
CPU: <model> (<cores> cores)
Memory: <sizeGB>GB
Computer built
```

## Notes

- Give `CPU` and `Memory` a way to describe themselves.
- Have `Computer` own both as **value members** and build the three output lines from those parts.

test_cases.label: Typical desktop
test_cases.expected_stdout:
CPU: Ryzen7 (8 cores)
Memory: 16GB
Computer built

test_cases.label: Single core minimal
test_cases.expected_stdout:
CPU: i3 (1 cores)
Memory: 4GB
Computer built

test_cases.label: Server config
test_cases.expected_stdout:
CPU: EPYC (128 cores)
Memory: 1024GB
Computer built

test_cases.label: Mid-range laptop
test_cases.expected_stdout:
CPU: M2 (10 cores)
Memory: 32GB
Computer built
